what_it_does: This file is a sparse attachment metadata record in channel memory: it declares an attachment id, its channel, an attachment_type of board_items, and a target board://item-3a049e77, plus an added timestamp.
observations: YAML frontmatter only, no body content
Fields: id, channel, attachment_type, target, added
target uses a board:// URI pointing to item-3a049e77
added timestamp is ISO 8601 with microsecond precision and UTC offset
standouts: board:// URI scheme for target reference
record is purely metadata with no payload
concerns: No payload or description of what is attached
Target board item is not defined or explained in this file
No author or source indicated for the added timestamp
notes: A minimal channel-memory stub that records a typed link from an attachment to a board item in the dragnet-development channel.
questions: What content or artifact does this attachment represent?
Is the attachment pointing to the board item, or is the board item pointing to the attachment?
What does attachment_type board_items signify semantically?
intention_signals: It appears to be trying to persist a timestamped, typed association between a channel attachment and a board item so the relationship can be retrieved later.
contribution: Provides a timestamped, typed link between a channel attachment and a board item, supporting traceability from attachment metadata to board content.
summary_for_embedding: A YAML-frontmatter channel attachment record mapping attachment att-e0f09df6 to board item item-3a049e77 in the dragnet-development channel with an added timestamp.
intention_for_embedding: To record a board-item attachment reference in channel memory for later lookup or provenance.
